mainEntity.name: Can I install Tailscale directly on a UniFi Dream Machine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. UniFi does not currently offer first-party native Tailscale support in the standard VPN settings, but the SierraSoftworks community project installs Tailscale on UniFi OS devices and runs it as a persistent service.
It can. Teleport is simple, but Tailscale is more flexible if you want subnet routing, exit nodes, ACLs, multi-site access, and support across non-UniFi devices.

mainEntity.name: Do I need to open ports to use Tailscale?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Tailscale allows access to internal resources without forwarding ports to the public internet.

mainEntity.name: Can I access VLANs over Tailscale?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Advertise each subnet you want reachable, then approve those routes in the Tailscale admin console.

mainEntity.name: Why does DNS fail after installing Tailscale on UniFi?

acceptedAnswer.text: In some setups, dnsmasq does not listen properly on tailscale0. Adding interface=tailscale0 to the dnsmasq config and restarting dnsmasq can fix local DNS resolution.

mainEntity.name: Why does the installer complain about Bullseye backports?

acceptedAnswer.text: Some UniFi OS builds reference Debian Bullseye backports in a way that can fail when the repository is no longer available from the expected mirror. Updating the source to the Debian archive path fixes the package lookup.
